Starting UIX Features

To start a UIX feature, call the ILiveEngine::StartFeature function. ILiveEngine::StartFeature requires that your title pass a structure containing the appropriate startup parameters for the feature being activated.

When a feature exits (whether successfully or unsuccessfully), or if the current network connection fails, ILiveEngine::DoWork sets the UIX_DOWORK_FEATURE_EXIT flag. Calling ILiveEngine::GetExitInfo indicates which feature exited, as well as the feature's exit status and return code.

Features are partitioned into discrete functional sets so that titles can choose to use the Xbox Drop-in UI for some features while implementing other functionality directly. Features are started with ILiveEngine::StartFeature, which takes a startup parameters structure that is dependent on the feature being activated.

  • Logon Feature
  • Friends Feature

Logon Feature

Calling ILiveEngine::StartFeature to start the logon feature displays the logon screen and begins the logon process. The pFeatureParams parameter must be a pointer to a UIX_LOGON_PARAMS structure. The information in this structure specifies the type of logon to perform, the maximum number of users to logon (1–4), the Xbox Live services that need to be accessed, and additional optional values for some logon types. Logon types include normal, silent, using a saved logon state, or responding to an invite.

Note    Titles using Xbox High-Level Voice (XHV) should initialize the voice engine (with XHVEngineCreate) only after the logon feature reports a successful logon.

After a successful logon (UIX_EXIT_LOGON_SUCCESSFUL), the title must call XOnlineGetServiceInfo for each service requested at logon in the LogonServiceIDs member of UIX_LOGON_PARAMS. The title checks the returned HRESULTs to verify that it has successfully connected to the services it requested. If a service critical to the title failed, the title should log off the Xbox Live service.

The following code fragment demonstrates how to start the logon feature.

UIX_LOGON_PARAMS ulp;
ZeroMemory( &ulp, sizeof(ulp) );
ulp.StructSize = sizeof(ulp);
ulp.LogonType = UIX_LOGON_TYPE_NORMAL;
ulp.LogonUserCount = 4;
ulp.LogonServiceIDs[0] = XONLINE_BILLING_OFFERING_SERVICE;
ulp.LogonServiceIDs[1] = XONLINE_SIGNATURE_SERVICE;
ulp.LogonServiceIDs[2] = XONLINE_MATCHMAKING_SERVICE;
pLiveEngine->StartFeature( UIX_LOGON_FEATURE, &ulp );

Friends Feature

Titles call ILiveEngine::StartFeature to start the friends feature. The friends feature displays players' friends lists, enabling players to invite other Xbox Live subscribers to play games. The pFeatureParams parameter must point to a UIX_FRIENDS_PARAMS structure that specifies the user (by port number) whose friends list is displayed, and the friend to initially select in the list (if any). Before starting the friends feature, a successful logon must have been performed. The friends feature also requires that XONLINE_MATCHMAKING_SERVICE was specified when the logon feature was started.

The following code fragment illustrates how to start the friends feature.

UIX_FRIENDS_PARAMS ufp;
ZeroMemory( &ufp, sizeof(ufp) );
ufp.StructSize = sizeof(ufp);
ufp.UserPort = 0;
ufp.SelectedFriendXUID.qwUserID = 0;
pLiveEngine->StartFeature( UIX_FRIENDS_FEATURE, &ufp );
